summary_one_line: Input needs a high-bit multibyte UTF-8 lead byte that decodes to a codepoint outside the allowed XML char range.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A byte at the parse cursor with the high bit set (c & 0x80) forming a multibyte UTF-8 sequence whose decoded value val fails IS_CHAR(val), e.g. a 0xf5/0xff lead-byte run that produces an out-of-allowed-range codepoint.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed begins with f5 ff ff ff ff ff ff ff: 0xf5 and 0xff have the high bit set, so c & 0x80 is true and the bytes are decoded as a multibyte sequence whose resulting val lands out of the IS_CHAR range, taking the blocked TRUE side and reaching the xmlErrEncodingInt call at lines 765-767.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The loser-blocking seed reaches line 764 but only on the False side (T=0 F=3): its decoded multibyte values stay inside the allowed XML char range, so IS_CHAR(val) is true and the TRUE branch is never taken.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds the I2SRandReplace mutator that value_profile lacks; I2SRandReplace splices logged comparand bytes into the input at the cursor offset, constructing the exact high-bit multibyte lead sequence that decodes to an out-of-range val, which value_profile's distance-feedback-only search fails to assemble within budget.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the IS_CHAR(val) test whose TRUE side is globally blocked.
Reaching line 764 requires a high-bit byte (c & 0x80) triggering the multibyte decode path.
The winner-resolving seed begins with a high-bit lead-byte run f5 ff ff ff ...
evidence_trail.exact_quote: f5 ff ff ff ff ff ff ff

evidence_trail.claim: The winner seed was discovered by value_profile_cmplog.
At the blocker line the loser only takes the False side (T=0) while the winner takes True.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 764  T=3 F=0  T=0 F=3  if (!IS_CHAR(val)) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The winner-resolving overlay shows the error-reporting block at 765-767 hit only by the winner.
value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace that value_profile lacks.
value_profile has no I2SRandReplace mutator, relying on CMP_MAP gradient only.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 8138 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed e77944c783da000c` and observed 2 I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 4 and 5 of the ancestor chain, characteristic of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find in vpc under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where value_profile resolves the IS_CHAR(val) TRUE branch as often and as fast as value_profile_cmplog, showing the out-of-range multibyte codepoint is reachable by CMP_MAP gradient alone without I2S substitution.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact decoded val that fails IS_CHAR is inferred from the high-bit lead bytes rather than observed directly; the precise out-of-range codepoint and which multibyte length path produced it is not pinned down in the prompt.
